# The Idaho Stack

> Regional publication and company map tracking Idaho’s technology economy: companies, people, infrastructure, AI, semiconductors, energy, agriculture, broadband, repair, workforce, and practical business technology.

## Core pages

- [Home](/): Publication front page and current editorial frame.
- [AI & Automation in Idaho](/ai-automation.html): First sector hub with adoption guidance, public-sector governance, workflow checklists, sector playbooks, map entries, and source links.
- [AgTech & Water in Idaho](/agtech-water.html): A practical Idaho agtech and water hub: irrigation, Snake River groundwater, food processing, water data, workforce, and what growers, processors, policymakers, and students should track.
- [Broadband & Infrastructure in Idaho](/broadband-infrastructure.html): A practical Idaho broadband hub: BEAD funding, rural and mountain connectivity, provider accountability, public infrastructure, outage resilience, and what businesses and communities should track.
- [Energy & Nuclear in Idaho](/energy-nuclear.html): A practical Idaho energy and nuclear hub: INL, Idaho Power planning, grid demand, advanced reactors, transmission, cybersecurity, storage, data centers, and workforce.
- [Repair & Hardware in Idaho](/repair-hardware.html): A practical Idaho repair and hardware hub: right-to-repair, device repair, farm equipment, business maintenance, e-waste, workforce skills, and technology that keeps working.
- [Semiconductors in Idaho](/semiconductors.html): A practical Idaho semiconductor hub: Micron, memory chips, CHIPS Act funding, Photronics, Boise State microelectronics, workforce, water, power, housing, and supplier constraints.
- [Idaho Business Technology Baseline](/business-technology-baseline.html): Practical scorecard for Idaho businesses deciding what to fix, implement, automate, document, secure, or leave alone.
- [Idaho Tech Briefing: What We’re Tracking First](/briefing-what-were-tracking-first.html): First briefing on Micron, INL, broadband, water, AI, repair, and workforce.
- [The Constraint](/the-constraint.html): Series intro on water, power, labor, housing, land, software, and time.
- [Idaho Tech Map v0](/companies.html): Source-linked directory of 38 seed entities.
- [Sectors](/sectors.html): AI, semiconductors, energy/nuclear, agtech/water, broadband, repair/hardware.
- [Regions](/regions.html): Treasure Valley, eastern Idaho, Magic Valley, north Idaho, Wood River Valley, statewide/rural.
- [Field Notes](/field-notes.html): Contributor format for firsthand observations.
- [Interviews](/interviews.html): Five Questions with Idaho Operators.
- [Submit](/submit.html): Tips, companies, corrections, field notes, events, and interview suggestions.

## Editorial rules

The Idaho Stack prioritizes specific Idaho evidence over smooth generic prose. Strong pieces name a place, company, public system, constraint, source, and next thing to watch.

- News and briefings: https://idahostack.com/news.html
- RSS feed: https://idahostack.com/feed.xml
- News sitemap: https://idahostack.com/news-sitemap.xml
- Anchor article: https://idahostack.com/articles/what-idahos-tech-economy-is-actually-made-of.html

## Latest articles

- [The Data Center Question Idaho Hasn’t Settled: Who Pays for the Power?](/articles/data-center-power-idaho-ratepayers.html): Analysis of Idaho Power, Micron, Meta, large loads, electric rates, and who carries grid-cost risk.
